citation_label: Income Tax (Amendment) Act, 2022 — rental Part VI substituted; s.22 rental deductions
heading: Income Tax (Amendment) Act, 2022 — rental Part VI substituted; s.22 rental deductions

metadata.act_number_note: Act 11 of 2022 (corroborated; not printed on the authenticated impression's face)
metadata.publisher: Uganda Printing and Publishing Corporation (UPPC), Entebbe
metadata.source_authority: Parliament of the Republic of Uganda
metadata.source_note: Income Tax (Amendment) Act, 2022 from the Parliament of Uganda CMIS repository (Clerk-authenticated printed impression with the President's signed assent page; OCR at 300 dpi). Section 5 (arrangement item; operative section numbered 5 in the body) amends s.22(1) so rental expenditure deductions apply only to persons other than individuals or partnerships, and the final section substitutes Third Schedule Part VI - the individual rental rate rows (nil to 2,820,000 per annum; 12% above) in force from 1 July 2022 through FY2025/26. The 2023 Revised Edition renumbers the part as Part II with identical rows (point-in-time consolidations at 2023-12-31 and 2024-12-23 held for diligence).
metadata.title: Income Tax (Amendment) Act, 2022 — rental Part VI substituted; s.22 rental deductions

body: Section 22 of the principal Act is amended in subsection (1)— (a) by substituting for paragraph (c), the following— “(c) in case of rental income, the expenditure and losses incurred by a person other than an individual or partnership in the production of such income subject to subsection (1a);”; (b) by repealing paragraph (ca); and (c) by inserting immediately after subsection (1), following— “(1a) Where the expenditure and losses incurred by a person other than an individual or partnership in the production of rental income exceeds fifty percent of the rental income, the allowable deduction shall be fifty percent of the rental income for that year of income.” 6. Amendment of section 85 of principal Act Section 85 of the principal Act is amended by inserting immediately after subsection (4), the following— “(5) For avoidance of doubt, income derived from the carriage of passengers who do not embark or cargo or mail which is not embarked in Uganda is not income derived from a Ugandan-source service contract.” 7. Amendment of section 89QA of principal Act Section 89QA of the principal Act is amended by substituting for subsection (1), the following— Act Income Tax (Amendment) Act 2022 “(1) Notwithstanding the provisions of sections 48 and 49A of the Tax Procedures Code Act, 2014, a licensee who fails to furnish a return or provide any other document within the time prescribed by this Act is liable to a penalty of not less than fifty thousand United States Dollars and not exceeding five hundred thousand United States Dollars.” 8. Amendment of First Schedule to principal Act The First Schedule to the principal Act is amended— (a) _ by inserting the following appropriately— “International Development Law Organisation (IDLO);”; and (b) by substituting for the words “Department for International Development (DFID)”, the words “Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO)”.

citation_label: Income Tax (Amendment) Act, 2020 (Act 20 of 2020) — Second Schedule presumptive rates substituted
heading: Income Tax (Amendment) Act, 2020 (Act 20 of 2020) — Second Schedule presumptive rates substituted

metadata.gazette_notification: Acts Supplement No. 12 to the Uganda Gazette, 27 November 2020
metadata.publisher: Uganda Printing and Publishing Corporation (UPPC), Entebbe
metadata.source_authority: Parliament of the Republic of Uganda
metadata.source_note: Income Tax (Amendment) Act, 2020 (Act 20 of 2020) from the Uganda Gazette Acts Supplement No. 12 of 27 November 2020 (archive.gazettes.africa scan of the gazette print; OCR at 300 dpi). Substitutes the Second Schedule small-business taxpayers tax rates: turnover bands 10m-30m / 30m-50m / 50m-80m / 80m-150m shillings with fixed amounts for taxpayers not keeping records and cumulative-component rates for taxpayers with records. In force for years of income from 1 July 2020 (URA operationalised the new rates a year late in its e-tax system, a compliance note only); unchanged through FY2025/26 (2021-2024 prints held for diligence).
metadata.title: Income Tax (Amendment) Act, 2020 (Act 20 of 2020) — Second Schedule presumptive rates substituted

body:
27th November 2020 2020 Act 20 Income Tax (Amendment) Act THE INCOME TAX (AMENDMENT) ACT, 2020 ARRANGEMENT OF SECTIONS Section Commencement 1. 4. 1 ACTS SUPPLEMENT No. 12 fl I ACTS SUPPLEMENT to The Uganda Gazette No. 77, Volume CXllt. dated 27th November, 2020 Printed by UPPC. Entebbe, by Order of the Government. I 2. Amendment of Income Tax Act 3. Amendment of section 22 of principal Act Insertion of sections 118G and 118H in principal Act 5. Amendment of section 122 of principal Act 6. Amendment of section 123 A of principal Act 7. Amendment of section 130 of principal Act 8. Amendment of First Schedule to principal Act 9. Amendment to Second Schedule to principal Act 10. Amendment to Third Schedule to principal Act

Act 20 2020 Income Tax (Amendment) Act THE INCOME TAX (AMENDMENT) ACT, 2020 Date of Assent: 24th November, 2020 Date of Commencement: 1st July, 2020 BE IT ENACTED by Parliament as follows: (a) 3 ! by inserting immediately after subsection (1) (w) the following— “(wa) the income ofthe Deposit Protection Fund established under section 108 of the Financial Institutions Act, 2016;”; An Act to amend the Income Tax Act, Cap. 340; to provide for incentives to existing investors; to exempt the Income of the Islamic Development Bank from Tax; to exempt the Income of the Deposit Protection Fund from Income Tax; to impose withholding tax on commissions paid to insurance and advertising agents; and for related matters. 1. Commencement This Act shall be deemed to have come into force on 1st July, 2020. 2. Amendment of Income Tax Act The Income Tax Act, in this Act referred to as the principal Act, is amended in section 21—

2020 (iii) (iv) (i) (ii) Act 20 Income Tax (Amendment) Act (b) by substituting for paragraph (af) the following— “(af) the income of an operator in an industrial park or free zone or the income of any other person carrying on business outside the industrial park or free zone and the investment capital of that operator or that other person, over a period of at least ten years from the date of commencement of business, is at least ten million United States Dollars, in the case of a foreigner or three hundred thousand United States Dollars, in the case of a citizen or one hundred fifty thousand United States Dollars, for a citizen whose investment is placed up country, or in the case of an existing operator or other person carrying on business outside the industrial park or free zone, from the date on which the operator makes an additional investment equivalent to ten million United States Dollars in the case of a foreigner or three hundred thousand United States Dollars in the case of a citizen, or one hundred fifty thousand United States Dollars, for a citizen whose investment is placed up country who, subject to availability, uses at least seventy percent of locally sourced raw materials and employs at least seventy percent of its employees being citizens earning an aggregate wage of at least seventy percent of the total wage bill and— processes agricultural goods; manufactures or assembles medical appliances, medical sundries or pharmaceuticals, building materials, automobile or household appliances; manufactures furniture, pulp, paper, printing and publishing of instructional materials; establishes or operates vocational or technical institutes; 4

Act 20 (c) by repealing paragraph (ag); (d) 1 = by inserting immediately after subsection (1) the following— (la) An operator in an industrial park or free zone or other person carrying on business outside the industrial park or free zone who undertakes investment and seeks to benefit from the income tax exemption provided under section 21(1) (ae) and (af) shall declare in their tax return for a year of income, the qualifying income and its related expenses. (lb) The qualifying income referred to in subsection (la), shall be the income attributable to the qualifying investment made in Uganda to be determined as follows— I *A/B Where: the sum of gross income and exempt income of the person for the year of income, before accounting for the qualifying income; A = the total amount of investment made by an investor from the beginning of the year of income in which the investment becomes a qualifying investment; B= the sum of the amount of the qualifying investments and the total investment made before the commencement of the current year ofincome; 5 Income Tax (Amendment) Act 2020 (v) carries out business in logistics and ware housing, information technology or commercial farming; or (vi) manufactures tyres, footwear, mattresses or toothpaste.”;

Act 20 44. (Id) For purposes of subsection (1) (af), the date of commencement of business of the investor shall be the later of the 1st day of the year of income or the first day during the year of income on which the qualifying investment generates income. (le) For purposes of subsection (lb), where a qualifying investment is still under construction or assembling, the amount in the qualifying investment shall be cumulated to the year of income in which the qualifying investment starts to generate income. E * F/G Where: E = the total allowable deductions for the year of income as provided for under this Act; F = qualifying income calculated under subsection (lb); G = the sum of gross income and exempt income of the person for the year of income, before accounting for the qualifying income. Income Tax (Amendment) Act 2020 (1c) The expenses which are related to the qualifying income shall be determined as follows: (n) expenses of a person who purchases goods or services from a supplier who is designated to use the e-invoicing system, unless the expenses are supported by e-invoices or e-receipts.” 6 3. Amendment of section 22 of principal Act Section 22 of the principal Act is amended in subsection (2) by inserting immediately after paragraph (m) the following—

2020 118H. 6. by renumbering section 123 A as subsection (1); (a) (b) 7 by inserting immediately after subsection (1), the following— Amendment of section 123A of principalAct Section 123 A is amended— “(2) A taxpayer who provides a passenger transport service or a freight transport service under subsection (1), shall be required to obtain a tax clearance certificate from the Commissioner in accordance with section 43 of the Tax Procedures Code Act, 2014 before renewal of operational licences.” Withholding of tax on commission paid to an advertising agent A person who makes payment for a commission to an advertising agent shall withhold tax on the gross amount of the payment at the rate prescribed in Part XIII of the Third Schedule.” 5. Amendment of section 122 of principalAct Section 122(ab) of the principal Act is amended by substituting for the words “section 118E” the words “section 118F”. “118G. Withholding of tax on commission paid to an insurance agent An insurance service provider who makes a payment of a commission to an insurance agent shall withhold tax on the gross amount of the payment at the rate prescribed in Part XIII of the Third Schedule. Act 20 Income Tax (Amendment) Act 4. Insertion of sections 118G and 118H in principalAct The principal Act is amended by inserting immediately after section 118F the following—

2020 “Second Schedule Tax rates with records Nil Nil 8 4 Where the gross turnover of the taxpayer exceeds ten million shillings but does not exceed thirty million shi 11 ings per annum Eighty thousand shillings Tax rates without records 0.4 % of the annual turnover in excess of ten million shillings i Gross turnover Where the gross turnover of the taxpayer does not exceed ten million shillings per annum “(5) A withholding agent who makes a payment subject to withholding tax under sections 83 to 86 and 117 to 119 shall furnish a return of withholding tax for every month in the Form specified by the Commissioner, not later than fifteen days after the end of every month to which the withholding tax relates.” “1. The amount of tax payable for purposes of section 4(5) is— Act 20 Income Tax (Amendment) Act 7. Amendment of section 130 of principal Act Section 130 of the principal Act is amended by inserting immediately after subsection (4) the following— 8. Amendment of First Schedule to principal Act The First Schedule to the principal Act is amended by inserting immediately after “International Telecommunications Union” the following— “Islamic Development Bank”. 9. Amendment of Second Schedule to principal Act The Second Schedule to the principal Act is amended by substituting for Part 1 and Part II the following Schedule—

Act 20 Income Tax (Amendment) Act 2020 >■ 2. (a) (b) .V ! 9 The tax payable by a tax payer under section 4 (5) is reduced by— Where the gross turnover of the taxpayer exceeds thirty mil lion shillings but does not exceed fifty million shillings per annum Where the gross turnover of the taxpayer exceeds fifty million shillings but does not exceed eighty million shillings per annum Where the gross turnover of the taxpayer exceeds Eighty million shillings but does not exceed one hundred and fifty million shillings per annum any credit allowed under section 128 (3) for withholding tax paid in respect of the amounts included in the gross turnover of the tax payer; or any credit allowed under section 111 (8) for provisional tax paid in respect of amounts included in the gross turnover of the tax payer.”. Two hundred thousand shillings Four hundred thousand shillings Nine hundred thousand shillings Three hundred and sixty thousand shillings plus 0.7% of the annual turnover in excess of eighty million shillings.” Eight thousand shillings plus 0.5% of the annual turnover in excess of thirty million shillings One hundred and eighty thousand plus 0.6% of the annual turnover in excess of fifty million shillings 10. Amendment of Third Schedule to principalAct The Third Schedule to the principal Act is amended—

by inserting immediately after Part XII the following— (b) “Part XIII 10 A Act 20 (a) Withholding tax rate on insurance and advertising agents The tax rate applicable to withholding tax for purposes of sections 118G and 118H is 10% of the gross amount of the payment.” Income Tax (Amendment) Act 2020 by substituting for the words “sports betting and pool betting” appearing in Part X, the words “betting or gaming”; and
